title: Text Inspection, Search, and Structured Transformation · Terminal Trace: Sort Uniq Locale
summary: Extract a defensible incident summary from mixed logs without silently corrupting delimiters, encodings, ordering, or duplicate policy. Planned artifact: Add and verify the linux sort uniq locale increment in a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Apply sort keys, numeric/version ordering, stable behavior, locale, and adjacent duplicate rules before using uniq.
Separate find traversal, predicates, grouping, pruning, printing, and actions while protecting path boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.shell:
# linux-basics cumulative operations evidence notebook

# Record hypothesis, command, expected signal, observation, conclusion, and recovery.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: Logs contain duplicate IDs, spaces, UTF-8 names, and severities high, low, and HIGH. Produce a stable 5-row summary with explicit locale, delimiter, regex, sort key, and duplicate policy. A defensible solution applies Sort Uniq Locale, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to a tested text-processing pipeline with fixtures.

content.code:
LC_ALL=C awk -F, 'NR > 1 { print $2, $1 }' data/incidents.csv \
  | sort -k1,1 -k2,2 \
  | uniq -c
